title: Cover-stop pressure and undercut-vulnerability features
summary: F1 strategy decisions are explicitly shaped by undercut/overcut pressure and competitors’ actions, and game-theoretic pit-stop work shows competition materially changes optimal decisions.
rationale: F1 strategy decisions are explicitly shaped by undercut/overcut pressure and competitors’ actions, and game-theoretic pit-stop work shows competition materially changes optimal decisions. The current row already contains contemporaneous field context (`PitStop`, position, lap pace) that can proxy whether a team should cover a rival on the next lap.
implementation_hint: Build strictly causal same-lap features such as number of nearby-position rivals who pitted this lap, field pit count on the lap, pace loss versus race/compound peers, and a simple clean-air or traffic-window proxy from position clustering; interact these with laps remaining and current compound.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on reactive stops that are triggered by rival behavior rather than by the driver’s own tyre-age window alone.
risk: Same-lap aggregates can accidentally leak if they use future ordering or target-derived info; keep them contemporaneous and preferably leave-one-row-out within lap aggregates.
